other_observers: Jason Beason, RMBO Daniel Gray, USFS Ryan Taylor, USFS David Bradford, USFS Albert Borkowski, USFS; Yes
specific_location: North side of building at Forest Service office, 403 N. Rio Grande Ave., Paonia.
description: The bird was slightly larger than house sparrows feeding at the same location. Plumage was typical of the "red" fox sparrow, including a rufous tail, rufous triangular spotting on a white to light gray breast, and an irregular rufous chest patch. Eyes were dark, bill was short and sharply pointed, yellow at the base and bottom and dark on the top surface. Head was gray and rufous, with faint rufous striping on the top of the head. Legs were dark dusty pink. Wings were gray/rusty with tips extending just past base of tail when folded.Behaviors: Bird was present at a ground feeding station at 0745 on 9 December 2009, accompanied by several darkeyed juncos and a spotted towhee. Bird was feeding on commercial birdseed mix. Bird fought with the towhee at one point early in the morning and was not run off by the towhee. Bird continued to feed on the ground throughout the day (unitl 1430 when this report was started), feeding for two to ten minutes then retreating to dense cover for ten to twenty minutes, then repeating process. I photographed the bird throughout the day.Call: None heard or recordedPlumage: typical red
photographer: Photographer: Dennis Garrison, Dennis Garrison, Dennis Garrison
